title: Shimano 2023-2024 Product Specifications Handbook
verified_scope: Shimano derailleur drivetrain tooth counts and compatibility limits
key_verified_facts: Official specification handbook supports the Shimano derailleur configurations used in the ratio benchmark, including CUES FC-U6000-2 36-22T with CS-LG400-11 11-45T and RD-U6020-11/FD-U6000 capacity closure; MTB 2x12 36-26T with 10-45T; trekking 3x configurations such as 44-32-22T with 11-34T and 48-36-26T with 11-36T; MTB 3x10 40-30-22T with 11-36T; and common Shimano road/gravel 2x examples.
limits_or_caveats: Derived total-range values are calculations from official tooth counts and derailleur capacity limits. They are not a single Shimano marketing claim and should not be mixed with unsupported cross-generation or over-capacity combinations.

title: SRAM Eagle cassette official product page
key_verified_facts: Official SRAM product page identifies a 10-52T Eagle cassette configuration; for a 1x derailleur drivetrain, cassette tooth spread determines the total ratio range when the same chainring is used.
limits_or_caveats: Absolute min/max drivetrain ratios still depend on the selected chainring. Use this only as a cassette-range benchmark unless a complete bike crank/cassette configuration is specified.

title: SRAM XPLR XG-1251 cassette official product page
key_verified_facts: Official SRAM product page identifies a 10-44T XPLR cassette configuration; for a 1x derailleur drivetrain, cassette tooth spread determines the total ratio range when the same chainring is used.
limits_or_caveats: Absolute min/max drivetrain ratios still depend on the selected chainring. Use this only as a cassette-range benchmark unless a complete bike crank/cassette configuration is specified.

title: Shimano Alfine SG-S7001-11 official product page
key_verified_facts: Official Shimano Alfine SG-S7001-11 page identifies the 11-speed internal gear hub and its 409% total gear range.
limits_or_caveats: Hub-internal range does not by itself define the whole-bike final drive ratio; sprocket and chainring selection still set absolute wheel-drive ratios.
